title: China-to-Indonesia BESS Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: PLN Grid Connection and ESDM Storage / Electricity Rules
rows.target_requirement: A grid-connected Indonesian BESS project is usually reviewed as an electricity project, not only as imported equipment. PLN interconnection requirements, the applicable grid code, protection settings, metering, dispatch interface, commissioning tests, and ESDM licensing or project rules can control whether the BESS may be energized. Storage treatment may vary by project role, such as generation support, renewable integration, captive power, utility procurement, or customer-side installation.
target_standards: PLN interconnection and technical approval requirements for the project
Applicable Indonesian grid code and protection, metering, and commissioning requirements
ESDM electricity licensing, renewable-energy, and project rules where storage is part of the permitted activity
rows.china_equivalent: China GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 evidence helps show battery safety, but PLN and ESDM review will also need site-specific electrical studies, grid models, protection settings, PCS behavior, communications, commissioning plans, and local permit evidence.
rows.gap: The gap is interconnection approval. Exporters should prepare single-line diagrams, grid studies, PCS certificates or test reports, frequency and voltage ride-through behavior where required, protection coordination, SCADA or EMS interface details, commissioning procedures, and Bahasa Indonesia project documents for PLN, ESDM, the owner, and the EPC.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected projects where PLN interconnection approval, grid-code compliance, ESDM licensing, or project approval applies; requirements are project-specific
mandatory_status.cn: China battery and BESS GB/GB-T evidence does not replace Indonesian PLN interconnection approval or ESDM project licensing

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat Indonesian grid connection as a separate PLN and ESDM approval workstream. Battery safety reports are necessary evidence but not a substitute for interconnection and project licensing review.

source.publisher: Kementerian Energi dan Sumber Daya Mineral Republik Indonesia

rows.target_requirement: IEC 62933 is an international electrical energy storage systems series often used to organize BESS terminology, planning, performance, and safety evidence. In Indonesia it should be treated as a project specification or engineering due-diligence framework unless it is called up by a contract, PLN requirement, lender, insurer, owner specification, or Indonesian regulation for the specific project. It is useful for integrating cells, racks, PCS, controls, fire response, protection settings, and operating procedures into one system-level safety case.
target_standards: IEC 62933 series — Electrical energy storage systems
IEC 62933-5 safety-related parts where specified by project documents
Indonesia project contract, lender, insurer, PLN, or owner technical specifications where they call up IEC 62933
rows.china_equivalent: China BESS packages commonly include GB 44240-2024 battery safety evidence and GB/T 36276-2023 lithium-ion battery evidence. Those documents are battery-focused and should be supplemented for Indonesia with system integration evidence such as PCS interface data, protection coordination, emergency operation, site layout, ventilation or gas controls where relevant, and commissioning procedures.
rows.gap: The gap is that battery-cell compliance does not equal system-level BESS acceptance. Prepare an IEC 62933-style technical file that links batteries, PCS, EMS, protection, communications, installation drawings, hazard controls, commissioning tests, and operating manuals to the Indonesian project requirements.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary unless incorporated by contract, PLN interconnection documents, lender or insurer requirements, owner specifications, or Indonesian regulation for the project
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 may be mandatory for covered China-market batteries; GB/T 36276-2023 and GB/T 36558-2023 are common technical references but do not replace Indonesian project acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Use IEC 62933 as the system-level evidence framework for Indonesian BESS projects, but verify whether it is actually mandatory in the contract, PLN review, insurance review, or regulation.

rows.requirement: K3L Safety, Health, and Environment Marking / Registration Check
rows.target_requirement: Indonesia's Ministry of Trade administers consumer-protection controls for goods related to safety, security, health, and the environment, commonly referred to as K3L. BESS units and industrial BESS sub-components are not currently confirmed within the mandatory K3L registration scope. Do not present K3L as a mandatory BESS approval unless the exact imported item is later added to the current K3L listed-goods scope or another binding Indonesian rule applies.
target_standards: Ministry of Trade K3L goods registration and supervision system
Importer responsibility for labels, manuals, and market surveillance only where K3L applies
rows.china_equivalent: China GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 reports may support technical safety claims for battery components, but they do not determine Indonesian K3L listing status, Indonesian-language labeling, importer registration, or market-surveillance obligations.
rows.gap: The gap is avoiding over-claiming. Keep HS codes, model lists, ratings, manuals, labels, safety warnings, battery hazard information, and Indonesian-language documents available for importer due diligence, but state honestly that BESS units and industrial sub-components are not currently in the mandatory K3L registration scope.

mandatory_status.target: Not currently mandatory for BESS units or industrial BESS sub-components because they are not presently in the mandatory K3L registration scope; re-check only if the listed-goods scope changes
mandatory_status.cn: China GB battery standards do not replace Indonesian K3L classification, marking, registration, or importer obligations

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] State the K3L position honestly: BESS units and industrial BESS sub-components are not currently in Indonesia's mandatory K3L registration scope. Do not represent China GB test reports as K3L registration or marking approval.

rows.requirement: Mandatory SNI IEC 62619 for Industrial / BESS Lithium-Ion Batteries
rows.target_requirement: Indonesia has made SNI IEC 62619 mandatory for secondary lithium cells and batteries for industrial applications, including BESS-relevant lithium-ion battery products, under Ministry of Industry Regulation (Permenperin) No. 41 Tahun 2019 as updated by Permenperin No. 69 Tahun 2024. The enforcement deadline for the updated mandatory scheme is 20 May 2025. Exporters should treat covered industrial/BESS lithium-ion batteries as requiring Indonesian SNI conformity through the applicable local certification route, while still checking whether a complete BESS unit has additional project, grid, fire, import, or component approvals.
target_standards: SNI IEC 62619 for secondary lithium cells and batteries for industrial applications
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly uses GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 for electric-energy-storage lithium-ion batteries. These GB documents can support the technical evidence package, but they do not prove that an Indonesian SNI adoption, SNI mark, project specification, or import requirement has been satisfied.
rows.gap: The gap is Indonesian mandatory SNI certification, not just technical testing. Exporters should prepare SNI IEC 62619 evidence, Indonesian importer details, battery ratings, safety data, factory/product traceability, and certification documents for covered industrial/BESS lithium-ion batteries before the 20 May 2025 enforcement deadline.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for covered industrial/BESS lithium-ion cells and batteries under Permenperin No. 41 Tahun 2019 and No. 69 Tahun 2024; updated enforcement deadline 20 May 2025
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-required depending on the China domestic scope for GB 44240-2024; GB/T 36276-2023 is commonly used as technical evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat SNI IEC 62619 as mandatory for covered industrial/BESS lithium-ion batteries in Indonesia under Permenperin No. 41 Tahun 2019 and No. 69 Tahun 2024, with the updated enforcement deadline of 20 May 2025. China GB battery reports do not replace Indonesian SNI certification.

rows.requirement: TKDN Local Content for Indonesian Procurement and Incentive Routes
rows.target_requirement: Indonesia's TKDN system measures domestic component level and is administered through Ministry of Industry systems. For BESS, TKDN is most important where a public procurement, PLN procurement, tariff or incentive route, local-content policy, or project contract requires a minimum domestic component level or proof of domestic production. Pure private import and sale may still need import and product compliance, but TKDN should not be described as a universal BESS product certificate unless the project route actually requires it.
target_standards: Ministry of Industry TKDN calculation and certification system
Public procurement, PLN procurement, tariff, incentive, or contract rules that require TKDN evidence
Project-specific local-content commitments for BESS components or services
rows.china_equivalent: China GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 evidence is unrelated to Indonesian local-content calculation. A technically compliant Chinese battery system may still fail a TKDN threshold if the procurement or incentive route requires Indonesian domestic content.
china_standards: Indonesia TKDN rules and Ministry of Industry calculation method
rows.gap: The gap is commercial eligibility rather than technical safety. Exporters should separate battery safety compliance from local-content planning, then document Indonesian assembly, cabinets, wiring, civil works, installation labor, software integration, testing, and after-sales service only where the TKDN method allows those elements to count.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only where procurement, PLN tender, incentive, tariff, local-content policy, or contract route requires TKDN evidence; not confirmed as a universal private-market BESS product certification
mandatory_status.cn: No China GB equivalent for Indonesian local content; GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 address technical battery safety, not TKDN eligibility

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] TKDN is a local-content and procurement eligibility issue. It should be checked early for PLN, public-sector, incentive, or contract-driven BESS projects, but it is not replaced by China GB safety evidence.

rows.requirement: Lithium Battery Transport — UN 38.3 and Dangerous Goods Documents
rows.target_requirement: Lithium cells and batteries shipped to Indonesia normally need transport documentation built around UN Manual of Tests and Criteria section 38.3, including a UN 38.3 test summary, classification, packing instructions, safety data, state-of-charge controls where applicable, and carrier-specific dangerous-goods documents for air, sea, or multimodal shipment. Indonesian import acceptance does not remove the separate obligations imposed by international transport rules and carriers.
target_standards: UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, section 38.3
UN Model Regulations dangerous-goods classification for lithium cells and batteries
Carrier and mode-specific dangerous-goods requirements for air and sea transport
rows.china_equivalent: China GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 reports address energy-storage battery safety, not dangerous-goods transport classification. A China safety report should be paired with a valid UN 38.3 test summary and shipment documents.
china_standards: UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, section 38.3
rows.gap: The gap is transport evidence, not product design alone. Exporters should prepare the UN 38.3 test summary, dangerous-goods declaration where required, packing evidence, battery handling instructions, emergency contact details, and shipping labels or marks before booking transport to Indonesia.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for lithium battery dangerous-goods transport where applicable under international mode and carrier rules used for shipment to Indonesia
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for export transport where lithium batteries are shipped as dangerous goods; China GB battery safety standards do not replace UN 38.3 transport evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Keep UN 38.3 and dangerous-goods shipping documents separate from BESS product-safety files. GB 44240 or GB/T 36276 evidence alone is not a transport clearance package.

source.publisher: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE)
